questions.text: A company wants to run a web application without managing the underlying servers, OS, or runtime. Which cloud service model best fits this requirement?

explanations.a: IaaS gives you VMs and networking but you still manage the OS, runtime, and middleware.
explanations.b: PaaS abstracts the underlying infrastructure so developers focus on code and data — the provider handles OS, runtime, and scaling. This is the correct answer.
explanations.c: SaaS is a fully delivered application (like Gmail) — the customer has no control over the code or runtime.
explanations.d: FaaS runs individual functions on-demand, not full applications with persistent state.

questions.text: Under the shared responsibility model in a public cloud IaaS environment, which element is the CUSTOMER responsible for?

explanations.a: Physical security of the data center is always the cloud provider's responsibility.
explanations.b: The hypervisor is managed by the cloud provider in IaaS — you never touch it.
explanations.c: In IaaS, the customer owns everything from the guest OS up: patching, configuration, and application security. This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: Physical network hardware is the provider's responsibility.

questions.text: A business can tolerate losing up to 4 hours of data in a disaster scenario. Which disaster recovery metric describes this requirement?

explanations.a: RTO defines the maximum acceptable time to restore service after a disaster — not data loss.
explanations.b: RPO defines how much data loss is acceptable, measured in time from the last good backup. A 4-hour RPO means backups must run at least every 4 hours. This is the correct answer.
explanations.c: MTTR is the average time to repair a failed component — not a DR planning metric.
explanations.d: MTBF measures the average time between failures — a reliability metric, not a DR metric.

questions.text: A DR site has all infrastructure pre-configured and data replicated in real time. It can take over within minutes. Which type of DR site is this?

explanations.a: A cold site is empty — it has power and connectivity but requires equipment and configuration before use, taking days to weeks.
explanations.b: A warm site has hardware and partial configuration but data is not fully current; failover takes hours.
explanations.c: A hot site mirrors the primary environment with live data replication and can fail over within minutes. This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: Cloud bursting is an elasticity pattern for handling demand spikes, not a DR site classification.

questions.text: Which storage type is best suited for storing large amounts of unstructured data such as images and log files, accessed via HTTP?

explanations.a: Block storage presents raw volumes to VMs — it is not HTTP-accessible and is not suited for large unstructured data at scale.
explanations.b: File storage uses a filesystem hierarchy (NFS/SMB) — it works for shared files but does not scale as cheaply as object storage for massive unstructured data.
explanations.c: Object storage (e.g., S3, Azure Blob) stores data as objects accessible via HTTP/REST, scales to petabytes, and is ideal for images, logs, and backups. This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: Archive storage is for rarely accessed data with retrieval delays — not for actively accessed images.

questions.text: A developer wants to run stateless event handlers that execute only when triggered, with no idle infrastructure costs. Which compute model fits best?

explanations.a: VMs are always running and incur cost even when idle — not suitable for event-driven, zero-idle-cost workloads.
explanations.b: Containers on always-on orchestration still consume resources at rest.
explanations.c: FaaS (serverless functions) runs code only on trigger events and charges per execution — zero cost when idle. This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: A dedicated host is a physical server reserved for one customer — maximum cost, never idle.

questions.text: Which cloud billing model provides the LOWEST cost for a production workload that runs continuously 24/7 with predictable resource needs?

explanations.a: On-demand is the most flexible but most expensive per hour — no discount for commitment.
explanations.b: Spot instances are cheapest but can be terminated with little notice — unsuitable for continuous production workloads.
explanations.c: Reserved instances offer up to 72% discount over on-demand in exchange for a 1- or 3-year commitment, making them ideal for steady, predictable workloads. This is the correct answer.
explanations.d: Dedicated hosts guarantee physical isolation but are the most expensive option.

questions.text: A VPC in region A needs low-latency connectivity to a VPC in region B from the same provider. Both VPCs use non-overlapping CIDR blocks. What is the most direct solution?

answers.text: VPN gateway between the two VPCs
Transit gateway with full mesh routing

explanations.a: A VPN gateway adds encryption overhead and latency — useful for on-premises connectivity, not optimal for same-provider VPC-to-VPC.
explanations.b: VPC peering creates a direct private network route between two VPCs with no bandwidth bottleneck or extra hops — the simplest and lowest-latency option for two VPCs. This is the correct answer.
explanations.c: A transit gateway is the right choice when connecting many VPCs, but adds unnecessary complexity for just two.
explanations.d: A CDN caches and distributes content to edge locations — it does not create private network connectivity between VPCs.
